response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with a genuine tooling and developer-experience background — including hands-on IDE product work on NI LabWindows/CVI and authoring test runners and assertion libraries — but the core stack for this role is largely absent. There is no Kotlin or Spring experience, Java appears only peripherally, and most recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than the JVM ecosystem. The IDE internals interest is real, but the required deep JVM, Spring, and enterprise-framework expertise is not demonstrated.
strengths: Direct IDE product development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authored language tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and low-latency systems work at scale
Broad polyglot experience across web, desktop, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems
concerns: No Kotlin or Spring ecosystem experience, both listed as required qualifications
Java exposure is minimal and dated; recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No evidence of deep JVM internals knowledge (runtime, build tools, application servers)

key_factors.factor: Proven seniority on complex, large-scale systems
